pattern: Represent text as two stacks split at the cursor: the left stack holds characters before the cursor and the right stack holds characters after, giving O(k) cursor movement and O(k) text operations.
hints: addText, deleteText, cursorLeft, and cursorRight all touch only the characters near the cursor position, not the whole text.
Model the cursor as a split between two halves of the text; moving left transfers one character from the left half to the right, and moving right does the reverse.
Two stacks (or deques) back-to-back at the cursor make every operation O(k) by popping from one and pushing to the other; the last-10-chars result is just the top 10 of the left stack.

edge_inputs.description: add text, delete some, add more, then move cursor both ways
cursor starts at leftmost; cursorLeft does nothing, returns empty string
cursor at right end; cursorRight does nothing
deleteText when fewer than k characters to the left; returns actual count deleted
cursorLeft returns min(10, chars-left); here text is shorter than 10
single character text; delete removes it; subsequent operations on empty editor
addText inserts at current cursor position (middle of text), not at end
cursorLeft partial move: fewer than k chars on left, cursor stops at 0

stress.description: random mix of addText, deleteText, cursorLeft, cursorRight on small alphabet text
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    import string
    ops = ['TextEditor']
    args = [[]]
    text_len = 0  # approximate left-side chars at cursor
    for _ in range(max(1, size)):
        r = rng.random()
        if r < 0.3:
            t = ''.join(rng.choice('abcde') for _ in range(rng.randint(1, 5)))
            ops.append('addText'); args.append([t]); text_len += len(t)
        elif r < 0.5:
            k = rng.randint(1, 5)
            ops.append('deleteText'); args.append([k])
            text_len = max(0, text_len - k)
        elif r < 0.75:
            k = rng.randint(1, 5)
            ops.append('cursorLeft'); args.append([k])
            text_len = max(0, text_len - k)
        else:
            k = rng.randint(1, 5)
            ops.append('cursorRight'); args.append([k])
            text_len += k  # approximate only, capped internally
    return [ops, args]
stress.note: small alphabet and short operations; checks cursor clamping and split-text consistency
